sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Le Havre has struggled recently, with no wins in their last 3 matches (1 loss, 2 draws), showing difficulty in scoring and defending. Stade Rennais, on the other hand, is unbeaten in their last 4 games (1 win, 3 draws), displaying better consistency and late-game scoring ability.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two head-to-head matches, Stade Rennais has dominated Le Havre, winning both games (1-0 and 5-1). Rennais has consistently scored first and maintained a strong defense against Le Havre.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Le Havre, sitting at 16th with 5 points, has scored only 6 goals this season (14th in league) and struggles with big chance creation (15th). Stade Rennais, at 9th with 9 points, shows a balanced attack and defense, with a tendency to score late in matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Trends
sections.text: Le Havre often concedes first (5/7 matches) and struggles to keep clean sheets against Rennais (6 matches). Rennais games frequently see over 4.5 yellow cards (5/6), while both teams’ matches tend to have under 10.5 corners.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Stade Rennais lead Le Havre by a score of 2-0 at Stade Oceane. The away side has been clinical in their approach, converting their chances with goals from Breel Embolo and Esteban Lepaul. Le Havre, despite showing some intent, have struggled to find the back of the net and will need a significant turnaround in the second half to get back into this match.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance analysis
halftimeSections.text: Stade Rennais have dominated possession and shown greater penetration in the final third, which is evident from their early lead. Their attacking players, particularly Embolo and Lepaul, have been a constant threat. On the other hand, Le Havre have had moments of promise, creating a couple of big chances but failing to capitalize on them. Defensively, they've been vulnerable, especially against quick transitions from Stade Rennais.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum insights
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half largely favored Stade Rennais during the opening stages, with sustained attacking spells that led to their first goal. Le Havre responded with a notable shift in momentum midway through the half, pushing forward with intent, but couldn't break through. Towards the latter part of the half, Stade Rennais regained control with another strong attacking phase, culminating in their second goal. Le Havre ended the half on the front foot with consistent pressure, which could carry into the second half if they maintain that energy.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Stade Rennais, Esteban Lepaul has been exceptional, contributing both a goal and an assist, making him a pivotal figure in their attack. Breel Embolo's finishing has also been crucial, and he remains a danger man. On Le Havre's side, Issa Soumaré stands out after converting a penalty and will likely be central to any potential comeback. Younes Namli has shown creativity in midfield for the home team and could be key in unlocking Stade Rennais' defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Le Havre will likely come out with renewed vigor, desperate to reduce the deficit early in the second half. Their late momentum in the first half suggests they can create chances, but they must be more clinical. Stade Rennais, with a comfortable lead, might adopt a more balanced approach, looking to exploit counter-attacks while managing the game. Given the dynamics, there’s a strong possibility of more goals as Le Havre push forward, potentially leaving gaps at the back.
